facts.text: Kingsbridge takes its name from a bridge erected in 1693 by enslaved Africans, later owned by Frederick Philipse, the lord of Philipsburg Manor.
The Kingsbridge Historical Society, founded in 1949, is the oldest local historical society in the Bronx and preserves the area’s rich heritage.
The Kingsbridge Armory, a red‑brick fortress built between 1912 and 1917 for the New York National Guard, dominates the neighborhood skyline.
A scene from Martin Scorsese’s The Irishman was filmed at the American Legion in Kingsbridge, linking the area to Hollywood history.
Kingsbridge transformed from a rural waystation into a suburb after the 1874 annexation of the western Bronx and the arrival of the New York Central Railroad.
A Carnegie library designed by McKim, Mead & White opened in 1905, showcasing early 20th‑century architectural heritage in Kingsbridge.
